content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Solve Deployment
content.seo_description: Learn how to use the Antigravity project template to overcome deployment hurdles. A high-impact guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template eliminates deployment friction by standardizing infrastructure-as-code and containerization patterns. By integrating Claude Code, you automate the complex environment configurations that typically stall student projects, allowing you to move from local development to a live, production-ready URL in under ten minutes.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks architecting your logic, refining your UI, and perfecting your data models. But when it comes time to ship, the 'it works on my machine' syndrome hits like a brick wall. The abyss between a local repository and a live, scalable application is where most student projects go to die, buried under a mountain of cryptic build errors and environment variable nightmares.

It is time to stop fighting the infrastructure and start shipping features. By utilizing the Antigravity project template, you bridge the gap between amateur coding and professional deployment. This isn't just about moving files to a server; it's about adopting a robust, repeatable framework that turns deployment from a terrifying mystery into a non-event.

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity Template
steps.description: Clone the base template to establish a professional folder structure and CI/CD pipelines immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard template with support for Next.js, Docker, and automatic Vercel deployment.

steps.title: Configure Environment Orchestration
steps.description: Standardize your .env handling to prevent deployment crashes due to missing keys.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure .env.example file and an orchestration script to validate all required variables before build time.

steps.title: Automate Dockerized Builds
steps.description: Containerize your application to ensure identical behavior across dev, staging, and production environments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a multi-stage Dockerfile optimized for small build sizes and rapid deployment cycles.

steps.title: Inject CI/CD Pipelines
steps.description: Set up automated testing and deployment hooks that trigger on every git push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, unit tests, and triggers a deployment to the cloud provider on merge.

steps.title: Final Deployment Validation
steps.description: Perform a dry-run deployment to catch configuration errors before they reach production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a final build check and verify that all production environment variables are correctly mapped.

pro_tips: Always use a separate 'staging' branch to test your build processes before merging into the production main branch.
Implement 'Build-Time Validation' in your CI/CD pipeline so that the build fails immediately if a key environment variable is missing.
Use ephemeral preview environments provided by platforms like Vercel or Railway to test your UI changes in a live setting before the final push.
Keep your Docker image slim by using .dockerignore to exclude node_modules and local logs from your final container layer.
Leverage Claude Code to write automated regression tests that run automatically every time your deployment script is triggered.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, focus on adding monitoring tools like Sentry or LogRocket to gain insights into real-world user performance.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the template to stop being a student developer and start being a professional engineer. Stop struggling with errors and start shipping value to the world. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
